description: Reference documentation for PFC contact model properties. Each contact model has specific properties that control its mechanical (or thermal) behavior. Use this reference when setting properties via 'contact cmat', 'contact property', or the Python SDK.

property_metadata_fields.description: Description including physical meaning and units
property_metadata_fields.modifiable: Can be changed after contact creation

models.name: linear
models.description: Linear elastic-frictional contact model with optional damping

models.name: linearcbond
models.description: Linear model with contact-based bonds for point adhesion

models.name: linearpbond
models.description: Linear model with parallel bonds for cemented materials - transmits force AND moment
models.common_use: Cemented particulate materials, intact rock, bonded assemblies

models.name: hertz
models.description: Nonlinear elastic contact model based on Hertz-Mindlin theory - F_n ~ δ^1.5
models.common_use: Impact simulations, nonlinear elasticity, realistic contact mechanics

models.name: rrlinear
models.description: Linear model with torque mechanism opposing rolling motion - simulates surface roughness effects
models.common_use: Granular materials with rolling resistance, heap repose angle analysis

models.name: arrlinear
models.description: The adhesive rolling resistance linear model is based on the rolling resistance linear model, to which an adhesive component is added. It is a linear-based mode
models.common_use: Cohesive granular materials with rolling resistance

models.name: bilinear
models.description: Built-in PFC contact model with a bilinear (two-branch) normal force-displacement response. Refer to the PFC 7.0 manual for the full property set and behavior.

models.name: burger
models.description: Burger’s model simulates creep mechanisms by using a Kelvin model and a Maxwell model connected in series in both the normal and shear directions.

models.name: eepa
models.description: Cohesion or adhesion can be modeled by introducing an attractive force component to the contact model. The terms cohesion and adhesion are usually defined for a

models.name: fish
models.description: The FISH contact model can facilitate contact model development. This model can be installed for any contact types, and is referred to in commands and FISH by t

models.name: flatjoint
models.description: The flat-joint model can be installed at both ball-ball and ball-facet contacts, and is referred to in commands and FISH by the name flatjoint .
models.common_use: Bonded-particle rock models with realistic strength envelope

models.name: hysteretic
models.description: The hysteretic contact model in PFC consists of a combination of the elastic portion of the Hertz model as described in the “ Hertz Model ” section, combined wi
models.common_use: Impact and collision with energy loss

models.name: jkr
models.description: Cohesion or adhesion can be modeled by introducing an attractive force component to the contact model. The terms cohesion and adhesion are usually defined for a

models.name: lineardipole
models.description: The linear dipole model is mechanically the same as the Linear Model , comprised of both linear and dashpot components. In addition to unbonded mechanical inter

models.name: mohr
models.description: The mohr model with inactive dashpots provides Mohr-Coulomb style slip with optional slip weakening, including dilation and pore pressure effects with healing.

models.name: null
models.description: The null model maintains a generalized internal force of zero ( F_c M_c 0 ). Newly created contacts are assigned this contact model unless an alternative contac
models.common_use: Disabling mechanical interaction at selected contacts

models.name: smoothjoint
models.description: The smooth-joint model can be installed at ball-ball contacts and is referred to in commands and FISH by the name smoothjoint .

models.name: softbond
models.description: The soft-bond model can be used to simulate both unbonded and bonded systems.
models.common_use: Bonded granular materials with post-peak softening

models.name: springnetwork
models.description: The spring network model can be used to simulate unbonded and bonded systems. The bonded formulation is based on the Rigid Body Spring Network (RBSN) lattice mo

models.name: subspringnetwork
models.description: The subspring network model can be used to simulate unbonded and bonded systems. Like the Spring Network Model, the bonded formulation is based on the Rigid Bod

models.name: thermalnull
models.description: The null thermal contact model is the default thermal contact model. No power is generated.
models.common_use: Disabling thermal conduction at selected contacts

models.name: thermalpipe
models.description: The thermal pipe contact model models a one-dimensional pipe with a thermal resistance per unit length of η .

notes: Property availability depends on the contact model
Inheritable properties can receive values from contacting pieces
Non-modifiable properties are read-only or calculated automatically
Use 'emod' and 'kratio' with deformability method for automatic stiffness calculation
Vector properties use contact plane coordinate system

Model availability is version-specific (see each model's 'availability'); browse with the 'version' parameter (6.0/7.0/9.0). Property content is shared across versions; properties added in a later version carry a 'since' marker.
Thermal contact models (thermalnull, thermalpipe) require the thermal option to be configured.
